Name: A multi-session simultaneous EEG-fMRI dataset with online experience sampling
Description: This dataset includes simulatenous EEG&fMRI and behavioral data collected form participants performing a sustained attention task and a resting state task with intermittent online multi-dimensional experience sampling.
Subjects: 24 healthy adults (64% female), ages 18-35 years (mean age: 21.17 years), all of whom were right-handed.
Funding: The National Institute of Mental Health (R21MH127384)
Acknowledgements: The authors thank Mike Esterman for providing the codes for the GradCPT task. We thank Julia Kam for providing guidance on creating a multi-dimensional experience sampling paradigm.
Ethics_Approvals: The study protocol was approved by the Drexel University Institutional Review Board. Written informed consent was obtained from each participant. All structural MRI images were defaced for privacy.
